findings.claim: A non-shared consumer (`var`) picker re-resolves its selection on every renderUI redraw via a fixed pipeline: read committed input `current=isolate(input[[id]])`, read boot `seed=isolate(state$spec_seed[[id]])`, compute `clear_for_new_source`, call `consumer_seed_decision(has_rendered, seed, current, node$default, cols, clear)`, set `extra$selected` ONLY if the decision's selected is non-NULL (else `invoke_build_ui` injects `node$default`), build the pickerInput which keeps `intersect(selected, cols)`, then flip the per-picker `has_rendered` latch iff `mark_rendered`.
findings.detail: Per-picker closure latches that persist across redraws: `has_rendered`, `last_src_identity`, `src_seen`. `boot_seed_selected` = (has_rendered ? current %||% character(0) : seed). The latch's intended meaning: "once settled, `current` is authoritative; seed is boot-only." invoke_build_ui uses `extra$selected` if present (even character(0)) and falls back to `node$default` only when `selected` is OMITTED — so returning `selected=character(0)` actively blanks the picker rather than re-showing the default.
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:2819-2868 (ptr_setup_consumer_uis renderUI); R/paintr-server.R:2482-2524 (consumer_seed_decision); R/paintr-server.R:2478-2480 (boot_seed_selected); R/paintr-build-ui.R:867-881 (invoke_build_ui); R/paintr-builtins.R:188 (intersect)
findings.verification: All 4 anchors present: consumer_seed_decision + boot_seed_selected defs (R/paintr-server.R), invoke_build_ui default-injection (extra$selected<-seed_default, R/paintr-build-ui.R), var-builder intersect (R/paintr-builtins.R). Line numbers stale post-merge; verified by symbol.

findings.claim: The `isolate()` around the consumer picker's own committed-input read is load-bearing: dropping it (to make the browser commit trigger a confirming redraw) causes an INFINITE reactive loop — the renderUI rebuilds the whole pickerInput each render, the fresh widget re-sends its value → input changes → re-render forever — so the app never reaches idle (AppDriver `wait_for_idle` times out).
findings.detail: Empirically A/B-verified: isolate dropped → never-stable; isolate restored → stable, reproduces the known bug, deselect contract holds. Therefore any "settle on confirmation" design (Option B) must confirm WITHOUT re-rendering the widget (e.g. updatePickerInput), not by removing the isolate.
findings.verification: `current <- shiny::isolate(input[[ns(raw_id)]])` present; OPTION-B experiment marker count=0 (revert confirmed). Infinite-loop claim is a this-session AppDriver experiment result (not statically in source) but the isolate anchor backs the 'load-bearing' claim.

findings.claim: `consumer_seed_decision` must gate the `has_rendered` latch flip on `seed_landed` (the seed's column actually present in `cols`, or an empty seed) — symmetric to the existing `default_landed` — instead of the old blanket `!is.null(selected)`, otherwise a seed whose column is not yet in `cols` flips the latch on the empty first render and is dropped by the downstream `intersect(selected, cols)`.
findings.detail: Also factor out `valid_current` (a current pick with ≥1 element in cols) and use it in `mark_rendered` so a never-landing/typo seed cannot trap the picker, WITHOUT letting `current` override the seed's `selected` (boot-only seed precedence preserved — locked by Seam 1c "seed wins over current"). This fix alone is necessary but NOT sufficient when a stage toggle also fires (see derived-column-seed-stage-toggle-race + option-i fallback).
findings.verification: seed_landed + valid_current present in consumer_seed_decision; 'Seam 1d' in test-spec-seed-boot-only.R; commit 4e6ee04 exists.

findings.claim: The derived-column picker is cleared at boot by a 3-redraw race: (1) column absent → defer; (2) column appears, seed lands, picker emits the seed and flips `has_rendered`; (3) a redraw fired by the boot-time stage toggle (`ggplot_N_stage_enabled=FALSE`, applied in `onFlushed`, invalidating the consumer's `state$stage_enabled()` dependency) arrives BEFORE the browser commits the seeded value back, so `current` is still NULL, `has_rendered` is already TRUE, and `boot_seed_selected` returns empty → picker blanks.
findings.detail: Empirically verified by instrumenting `consumer_seed_decision` in an AppDriver fixture. Removing only the stage toggle leaves 2 redraws and the seed survives. `mpg` (real column) is immune because it lands at redraw 1, committing before the toggle redraw. The latch's assumption — "after settling, `current` reflects the emitted value" — is false in the emit→commit gap.
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:2726-2742 (entry_reactive deps incl state$stage_enabled()); R/paintr-server.R:683-695 (apply_spec_at_boot onFlushed dispatch of stage/checkbox); R/paintr-server.R:2841 (current isolate)
findings.verification: state$stage_enabled() is read twice inside ptr_setup_consumer_uis (consumer renderUI dep); apply_spec_at_boot onFlushed present. Mechanism also empirically demonstrated this session via instrumented AppDriver trace. Line refs stale post-merge.

findings.claim: An AppDriver boot/runtime claim must run an app DIRECTORY whose `app.R` first line is `pkgload::load_all(Sys.getenv("GGP_PKG"), ...)` with the test setting `GGP_PKG=normalizePath(test_path("..",".."))` and `NOT_CRAN=true`; instrument internal behavior by `assignInNamespace()`-wrapping a function CALLED BY BARE NAME (e.g. `consumer_seed_decision`) — wrapping a function whose reference the registry captured at registration (e.g. `ptr_builtin_var_build_ui`) does NOT take effect.
findings.detail: Verified this session: the var-builder wrapper never fired (registry holds the original ref), but the `consumer_seed_decision` wrapper did (it is looked up by name at each call). `app$get_logs()` surfaces child-process `cat()` output for retrieval.
findings.source: tests/testthat/fixtures/vignette-apps/*/app.R first line pkgload::load_all(Sys.getenv('GGP_PKG')); probe pattern used this session
findings.verification: 84 fixture app.R files use pkgload::load_all(Sys.getenv('GGP_PKG'),...); it sits at line ~7 after the comment block (NOT literally line 1 -- minor wording imprecision). assignInNamespace-by-bare-name probing is a process technique used this session, consistent with .claude/rules/serena-tools.md child-process instrumentation guidance.

findings.claim: dev/agents/ggpaintr-source-expert/symbol-lines.json is a GENERATED artifact (rebuilt by build-symbol-manifest.R via the pre-commit hook); a merge conflict in it must be resolved by re-running that script against the merged source and staging, NOT by hand-merging the JSON.
findings.detail: Merging ppUpload-bug into eyeball-play auto-merged R/paintr-server.R cleanly (Option I non-shared producer loop and the host-scope clear-latch occupy different regions of the same function file) and ONLY symbol-lines.json conflicted. Pre-commit also regenerates it on the merge commit anyway.
findings.verification: build-symbol-manifest.R generator exists; pre-commit-symbol-manifest.sh invokes it; symbol-lines.json regenerable. Confirmed during the 0cb1efd merge (only that file conflicted; R/paintr-server.R auto-merged).

findings.claim: The complete fix ("Option I") is: in the consumer's column-list reactive, when an upstream producer's value is NULL (input not yet committed), fall back to its boot value — `isolate(state$spec_seed[[pid]])` else the producer node's deparsed `$default` — so a derived column exists in `cols` from the FIRST redraw and the picker behaves like a real-column picker (seed lands + latch flips at boot, before any stage-toggle redraw).
findings.detail: Fallback fires ONLY on `is.null(val)`; a user-cleared expression commits `""` (not NULL) and is respected, so the seed is not re-injected over an intentional clear. Leaves the latch/deselect logic untouched (deselect contract pinned green). Behavioral ripple: derived columns now appear in consumer choices at boot rather than one redraw late.
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R consumer column-list reactive producer loop (~2751-2762 + per-consumer upstream_producer_defaults map); commit 1a7644c; tests/testthat/test-spec-seed-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R; tests/testthat/test-default-derived-column-stage-off-browser.R
findings.verification: Non-shared producer loop falls back to isolate(state$spec_seed[[pid]]) %||% upstream_producer_defaults[[pid]]; the defaults map is built at R/paintr-server.R:2689-2694, used ~2776; 'Option I (2026-05-30)' comment present; commit 1a7644c exists.

findings.claim: A bare `expect_equal(app$get_value(input=id), <seed>)` on a source-headed or host-scope renderUI consumer picker is boot-tail-flaky under the parallel gate (get_value returns NULL because wait_for_idle() can return before the client round-trips the seeded value, and parallel CPU contention stretches that lag to seconds); fix is the existing `expect_input_eventually(app, id, expected)` polling helper, which only adds tolerance.
findings.detail: Post-merge parallel gate (TESTTHAT_CPUS=4) showed FAIL 1 / SKIP 0 / PASS 3438 reading "hp" as NULL; both candidate tests passed clean serially, confirming a flake not a regression (per CLAUDE.md "re-confirm serially before calling it a regression"). wait_for_idle present in the test was insufficient for a source-headed picker. The parallel `progress` reporter only flushed the final failure summary (no per-test name in captured stdout) -- identify the culprit by grepping the suite for the asserted literal.
findings.verification: expect_input_eventually defined at helper-vignette-apps.R:114; test-default-seeding-with-source-upstream.R uses it 3x; test-e2e-vignette-examples-shinytest2.R uses it 1x (shared_col); commit a9fd8cb exists.

findings.claim: A producer placeholder's (ppExpr/ppNum/ppText) value reaches the server pipeline only after TWO delays — the inherent Shiny client→server round trip (input is client-populated; renderUI/updateXInput cannot set input directly) PLUS an explicit `shiny::debounce(reactive(input[[id]]), 300ms)` — and `substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value` resolves a producer ONLY from the committed `ctx$snapshot`, with NO fallback to `node$default` or the spec seed.
findings.detail: Consequence: a column DERIVED from a producer (`mutate(adj = ppExpr(...))`) does not exist on the server at the first consumer redraw (ncol unchanged); it appears one redraw later once the expression box commits + debounce elapses. A real (non-derived) column is in `cols` from redraw 1, which is why real-column seeds never hit this race.
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:1675 (shiny::debounce of input); R/paintr-server.R:1693 (.PTR_DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_WINDOW <- 300L); R/paintr-substitute.R:90-128,262 (substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value reads only ctx$snapshot)
findings.verification: shiny::debounce( of input present; .PTR_DEFAULT_DEBOUNCE_WINDOW <- 300L present; substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value reads via read_placeholder_value(node, ctx) (snapshot only), no node$default fallback.

findings.claim: ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis builds its ptr_resolve_upstream snapshot ONLY from data-source ids (panel sources / shortcut / self) and never reads upstream producer values -- but this is consistent with (not a separate bug from) the shared resolver already having truncated producers out of resolution$value upstream, so there are no producers there to feed.
findings.detail: Correction of an earlier same-session hypothesis that called this a "deeper bug": the producer-blindness of the snapshot is moot because the resolver truncates producer-bearing stages before this renderUI ever runs. The host consumer binder runs at state=NULL (host scope, ADR 0006) and at state!=NULL (single app / per-instance) in one function.
findings.verification: Inside ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis, snap[[...]] is written only for source ids (shortcut_id/sid/cmp); grep for producer_input within the function = 0. CORRECTION of an earlier same-session over-claim: this is moot, not a bug, because the resolver truncates producers out upstream (see shared-consumer-truncate-at-placeholder-intentional).

findings.claim: A shared consumer's column list is INTENTIONALLY truncated at the first upstream stage containing any placeholder and fed the source columns -- so a column derived by a placeholder-carrying stage (`mutate(adj = ppExpr(...))`) is NEVER offered to a `var(shared=)` picker, by design, NOT a bug.
findings.detail: Documented at R/paintr-shared.R:89-127 with that exact mtcars/adj example and "intentional, not an oversight". Two constraints force it: (a) the framework must never invoke a placeholder hook (a custom resolve_expr may do I/O / open DB) just to populate a dropdown; (b) divergent occurrences have no canonical occurrence to source producer values from. Rule of thumb in the doc: to reflect a pipeline's own derived columns, do NOT declare the var `shared`. Probe: ptr_resolve_shared_consumers for the shared key returns 0 producer nodes and cols = names(mtcars). The non-shared Option I fix is correct precisely because the non-shared path has ONE canonical occurrence with live producer values; porting it to shared would violate (a)/(b). Pinned by test-shared-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R (commit 5e3a748).
findings.source: R/paintr-shared.R:65-127 (design comment); R/paintr-shared.R:131-211 (contains_placeholder, truncate_upstream_at_placeholder, extract_source_leaf); R/paintr-shared.R:276-317 (resolve_shared_consumer)
findings.verification: 'This is intentional, not an oversight.' comment at R/paintr-shared.R:104; contains_placeholder (131) + truncate_upstream_at_placeholder (180) defs present. Empirically probed this session: resolution has 0 producer nodes, cols=names(mtcars).

findings.claim: A producer (ppExpr/ppNum/ppText, the "value" role) CAN legally take `shared=` (validated only by ptr_validate_shared_roles, which just requires all placeholders sharing ONE key to be the same role), and shared value widgets bind once per key at canonical id `shared_<key>` -- but the shared-consumer truncation never inspects `$shared`, so a shared ppExpr in the upstream is still truncated away and a downstream `ppVar(shared=)` does NOT see its derived column.
findings.detail: ppUpload/ppExpr/ppVar must use DIFFERENT keys (a source+value+consumer cannot share one key -> abort "incompatible roles"). The user's "bundle" mental model (shared ppUpload+ppExpr feeding a shared ppVar's upstream) is a coherent design EXTENSION not implemented today: a shared producer has a canonical value, which dissolves constraint (b), but constraint (a) (don't run custom hooks for a dropdown) still applies. Making truncation shared-aware is estimated ~3-4 sessions / 150-300 LOC across paintr-shared.R + paintr-server.R + an ADR reversal, with the truncation rewrite the high-risk hotspot; descope to built-in-producers + single-occurrence ~1.5-2 sessions.
findings.source: R/paintr-translate.R:1066 (ptr_validate_shared_roles); R/paintr-server.R:2213+ (ptr_setup_value_uis shared-value loop); R/paintr-shared.R:131-211 (truncation ignores $shared)
findings.verification: ptr_validate_shared_roles at translate.R:1066 with 'A shared widget can only back one role at a time.' (1081 - same-role-per-key rule); shared value widgets bind once per key (paintr-server.R:2150 'Shared value widgets bind ONCE'); truncation funcs do not inspect $shared. Empirically: the all-shared chain translates, resolution still has 0 producers.
